mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above its 12-month trend, equity investors anticipate future monetary tightening. This expectation compresses forward equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices risk premia.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during low-rate inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.5% signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures. This reduces corporate profit margins and increases discount rates, producing negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE < 6.5%, or correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and forward economic momentum. This signals sustained earnings growth and justifies higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1669k units, RSAFS at 540.9B as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: Rank IC between joint HOUST-RSAFS signal and 20-day returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are negative in more than 50% of high-demand periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience. This reduces inflation expectations and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment improves.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 1% MoM are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k per month while the unemployment rate remains elevated above 6.0%, signaling a stalled labor market recovery. This reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142.6M, UNRATE at 6.3% as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: weak labor market recovery with stalled momentum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the joint signal and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2% annualized, indicating robust economic expansion. This supports corporate earnings growth and justifies higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are confirmed.
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held constant near zero while real yields (implied by CPIAUCSL inflation) turn negative, equity investors view stocks as an inflation hedge. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as capital rotates into equities from fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09%, CPIAUCSL at 262.23 as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in negative real-yield regimes are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, signaling stagflationary pressures. This compresses equity valuations as investors reduce growth expectations and demand higher risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.8%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.72, CPIAUCSL at 262.23 as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of stagflationary episodes, or Rank IC between the joint signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
